component.name: Timeline
component.description: Chronological event list with vertical or horizontal orientation. Each item supports a date, title, content body, and a customizable indicator (dot, icon, or numbered circle). Vertical mode supports an alternating layout with items zig-zagging across the centerline.

useCases: Order tracking (e.g. shipped → in transit → delivered)

commonPatterns.name: vertical-with-dates
commonPatterns.description: Default vertical orientation with date on the left and title/content on the right
commonPatterns.composition:
<Timeline>
  <TimelineItem step={1}>
    <TimelineIndicator />
    <TimelineDate>Jan 12</TimelineDate>
    <TimelineTitle>Account created</TimelineTitle>
    <TimelineContent>Welcome email sent.</TimelineContent>
  </TimelineItem>
  <TimelineItem step={2} last>
    <TimelineIndicator />
    <TimelineDate>Jan 13</TimelineDate>
    <TimelineTitle>Profile completed</TimelineTitle>
  </TimelineItem>
</Timeline>

commonPatterns.name: horizontal
commonPatterns.description: Horizontal orientation for compact roadmaps or order-tracking strips

commonPatterns.name: alternating-layout
commonPatterns.description: Items zig-zag across the centerline — common pattern for marketing or roadmap pages

commonPatterns.name: icon-indicator
commonPatterns.description: Use children inside TimelineIndicator to show an icon or number instead of a dot

commonPatterns.name: subtle-dot
commonPatterns.description: Use variant='dot' for a minimal, non-emphasized event marker

antiPatterns.scenario: Using Timeline for an actionable multi-step flow
antiPatterns.reason: Timeline is retrospective/chronological; Steps is procedural with status tracking
antiPatterns.alternative: Use Steps for forms, wizards, and flows where the user is moving through stages

antiPatterns.scenario: Forgetting last on the final item
antiPatterns.reason: The connector line continues past the last indicator, leaving a dangling line
antiPatterns.alternative: Always pass last on the final TimelineItem

antiPatterns.scenario: Using alternating mode with horizontal orientation
antiPatterns.reason: alternating only affects vertical layouts — has no effect horizontally

antiPatterns.scenario: Mixing dot and outline-circle indicators inconsistently in one Timeline
antiPatterns.reason: Visual inconsistency makes the timeline harder to scan
antiPatterns.alternative: Pick one indicator style per Timeline; use children only when you need an icon

semanticMeaning.vertical: Top-to-bottom event list (default). Best for long histories.
semanticMeaning.horizontal: Left-to-right strip. Best for short, fixed-step sequences (e.g. order tracking).

slots.indicator: TimelineIndicator — dot, outline circle, or icon-bearing circle (children render inside)
slots.date: TimelineDate — small muted text, positioned left in vertical (right of centerline) or above in horizontal
slots.title: TimelineTitle — primary label-md text
slots.header: TimelineHeader — alternative wrapper for grouping label-style children (used in alternating layouts)
slots.content: TimelineContent — body paragraph for additional detail

interactions.static: Timeline is non-interactive by default; interactive content goes inside TimelineContent or TimelineHeader

responsive.mobile: Vertical orientation works well; horizontal can overflow — consider vertical on small screens
responsive.desktop: Either orientation works; alternating layout is most effective at md+ where there is room for the zig-zag

accessibility.role: Renders as <ol>; each TimelineItem renders as <li> with data-orientation
accessibility.keyboardSupport: Not interactive by default — focus is managed by any interactive children
accessibility.screenReader: Reads as an ordered list; date, title, and content are read in DOM order
accessibility.focusManagement: No internal focus logic; managed by interactive children

rules: Always set last on the final TimelineItem to hide the trailing connector
Pass step={n} on each TimelineItem so the connector logic and alternating layout know the position
Use orientation='horizontal' only for short, fixed sequences (4-6 items max); vertical scales better
alternating only applies to vertical orientation
Use TimelineIndicator with children for icon-bearing markers, variant='dot' for subtle markers, or no children for the default outline-circle marker
Pair with Avatar in TimelineHeader for activity-feed patterns (who did the thing)

aiHints.context: Use for chronological lists of events that have already happened or are scheduled across time. Prefer Steps for procedural flows where the user is actively moving through stages. Vertical orientation suits long histories; horizontal suits fixed short sequences like order tracking. Set last={true} on the final item.
